items.title: Support state bills S1022/H.1559 and H.4835 for pet-friendly housing and pet parity in elderly housing
items.summary: Council resolution endorsing two pieces of state legislation: S1022/H.1559 (an Act to maintain stable housing for families with pets) and H.4835 (an Act to codify pet-friendly elderly housing policies and ensure pet parity across housing authorities). Adopted as amended by voice vote.
items.body: City Council support of S1022/H.1559, An Act to maintain stable housing for families with pets, and H.4835, An Act to codify pet-friendly elderly housing policies and ensure pet parity across housing authorities. COUNCILLOR MCGOVERN, COUNCILLOR SOBRINHO-WHEELER, MAYOR SIDDIQUI, COUNCILLOR SIMMONS. POR 2026-56
amendments: Mayor Siddiqui added as sponsor in Final Actions (not on Agenda)

items.title: Raise residential parking permit fee to $75, $25 for low-income; remove senior exemption; cap at 2 cars/household
items.summary: A previously-tabled policy order proposing major changes to the residential parking permit program: a $75 base fee, a subsidized $25 fee for residents in affordable housing or SNAP, removal of the existing senior exemption, and a cap of 2 permits per household (down from 4). The item remained on the table at this meeting; no action was taken at March 23, 2026.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant City departments to raise the fee of the parking permit program for all residents to $75, consider how to include a self-identified check off option so as not to increase administrative costs for a subsidized fee of $25 for residents who live in affordable housing, are enrolled in a program such as SNAP or are low income, remove the senior exemption for the residential parking permit program and lower the number of cars that individual residents are allowed to get a residential parking permit for from four to two. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL FEBRUARY 9, 2026. PLACED ON THE TABLE IN COUNCIL MARCH 2, 2026. POR 2026-32

history.action: Charter Right Exercised by Councillor Simmons

rationale.doc.kind:POR: Section VII. CALENDAR / TABLED — original POR
rationale.doc.meeting_date:2026-03-16: Item appeared on the March 16, 2026 agenda
rationale.doc.stage:tabled: PLACED ON THE TABLE IN COUNCIL MARCH 2, 2026; appeared in the TABLED section
rationale.doc.sponsor:simmons: Charter Right Exercised by Councillor Simmons indicates her as primary mover prior to tabling
rationale.geo.scope:citywide: residential parking permit program applies citywide
rationale.housing.tenure:any: residential parking permits are available regardless of tenure
rationale.housing.affordability:inclusionary: residents who live in affordable housing
rationale.fiscal.kind:parking_fee: raise the fee of the parking permit program
rationale.demo.age:senior_65plus: remove the senior exemption for the residential parking permit program
rationale.demo.income:low: subsidized fee of $25 for residents who ... are low income
rationale.demo.income:means_tested: enrolled in a program such as SNAP

items.title: Zoning 4.50 amendment: childcare, religious, and educational uses as-of-right citywide (Unfinished Business)
items.summary: An ordinance amending Cambridge Zoning Ordinance Section 4.50 to allow religious uses, qualifying educational uses, and child-care uses (centers, school-age programs, family child care homes) as-of-right in every zoning district. Pending as Unfinished Business; eligible to be ordained March 23, 2026. No action taken at the March 23 meeting.
items.body: AMEND SECTION 4.50 OF THE CAMBRIDGE ZONING ORDINANCE AS FOLLOWS WITH THE INTENT OF PERMITTING THE USE OF LAND FOR THE FOLLOWING PURPOSES AS-OF-RIGHT IN ALL ZONING DISTRICTS: RELIGIOUS PURPOSES; EDUCATIONAL PURPOSES ON LAND OWNED OR LEASED BY THE COMMONWEALTH OR ANY OF ITS AGENCIES, SUBDIVISIONS OR BODIES POLITIC OR BY A RELIGIOUS SECT OR DENOMINATION, OR BY A NONPROFIT EDUCATIONAL CORPORATION; AND FOR A CHILD CARE CENTER, SCHOOL-AGED CHILD CARE PROGRAM, FAMILY CHILD CARE HOME, OR LARGE FAMILY CHILD CARE HOME AS DEFINED IN SECTION 1A OF CHAPTER 15D OF MASSACHUSETTS GENERAL LAWS. PASS TO A SECOND READING IN COUNCIL MARCH 2, 2026. ELIGIBLE TO BE ORDAINED MARCH 23, 2026. ORD 2026-01

rationale.doc.kind:ORD: Section VII. CALENDAR / UNFINISHED BUSINESS — ordinance
rationale.doc.stage:second_reading: PASS TO A SECOND READING IN COUNCIL MARCH 2, 2026
rationale.geo.scope:citywide: AS-OF-RIGHT IN ALL ZONING DISTRICTS
rationale.housing.tenure:any: zoning amendment touches all property uses
rationale.household.kids:under_5: CHILD CARE CENTER, FAMILY CHILD CARE HOME
rationale.topic.area:housing_zoning: AMEND SECTION 4.50 OF THE CAMBRIDGE ZONING ORDINANCE
rationale.topic.area:schools: educational purposes and school-age childcare uses

items.title: Open city-owned parking lots, including Tobin/Darby Vassal complex, for after-hours resident use
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to coordinate with the School Department, DPW, and other departments to make all city-owned parking lots — with a focus on school complexes including the still-under-construction Tobin/Darby Vassal lot — available for after-hours use by residents.
items.body: That the City Manager is requested to work with the School Department, the Department of Public Works, and other relevant departments to ensure that all city owned parking lots, with a focus on school complexes, including the still under construction parking at Tobin/Darby Vassal school complex, could be made available for after-hours use by residents. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-29. Councillor Nolan. AR 2026-02

items.title: Establish City policy on role and financial responsibilities for large-scale public events by nonprofits
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to consult with the City Solicitor's Office and other departments to establish a formal policy defining the City's role and financial responsibilities in supporting large-scale public events hosted by Cambridge-based nonprofits and not-for-profits.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to consult with the appropriate City departments, including the City Solicitor's Office, to establish a formal policy that clearly defines the City's role and financial responsibilities in supporting large-scale public events hosted by Cambridge-based non-profit and not-for-profit organizations. Original Awaiting Report Item AR-25-48. Councillor Simmons, Councillor McGovern. AR 2026-03

items.title: Review legal/oversight framework for Tourism Destination Marketing District and visitor information kiosk
items.summary: Awaiting Report directing the City Solicitor and CDD to report on (1) legal parameters for City funding and Tourism Destination Marketing District restrictions and (2) oversight and accountability for the visitor information kiosk's operation, including alignment with City priorities.

items.title: Pilot pedestrianization projects in 2026, including Brattle Street two-way restoration to enhance JFK Square
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to prioritize pedestrianization pilot projects in 2026 (without an extensive consultant study), and in the medium term to consider how additional pedestrianization of a section of Brattle Street — by restoring two-way traffic to JFK Street — could enhance Harvard Square.
items.body: That the City Manager work with relevant City departments to prioritize pilot projects in 2026 without the use of a consultant for an extensive pedestrianization study, and in the medium-term consider how additional pedestrianization of a section of Brattle Street by restoring two-way traffic to JFK Street could enhance the Square. Councillor Nolan, Councillor Simmons, Mayor Siddiqui, Councillor Zusy. AR 2026-12

items.title: Comprehensive review of safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to conduct a comprehensive review of existing safety and security measures at Cambridge City Hall, including physical access controls, visitor screening, on-site security staffing and training, emergency response and evacuation, and surveillance systems.

items.title: Examine whether City can prevent CPD hiring of officers who worked for ICE/HSI/CBP
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to direct staff to examine whether and how the City may prevent the Cambridge Police Department from hiring any sworn officer who was hired by Immigration and Customs Enforcement, Homeland Security Investigations, or Customs and Border Patrol.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to direct the relevant City staff and departments to examine if and how the City may prevent the hiring by the Cambridge Police Department of any sworn officer who was hired by Immigration and Customs Enforcement, Homeland Security Investigations, or Customs and Border Patrol, and report back to the City Council in a timely manner. Councillor Sobrinho-Wheeler, Councillor McGovern, Councillor Al-Zubi. AR 2026-15

items.title: Examine third-party guarantors/co-signers for Inclusionary Housing applicants
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the Community Development Department to examine the feasibility, legal considerations, and administrative requirements of permitting qualified third-party guarantors or co-signers for applicants to Inclusionary Housing units, and to recommend whether such a policy could responsibly expand access.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to direct the Community Development Department (CDD) to examine the feasibility, legal considerations, and administrative requirements of permitting qualified third-party guarantors or co-signers for applicants to Inclusionary Housing units and provide recommendations as to whether such a policy could responsibly expand access to these units. Vice Mayor Azeem, Councillor McGovern, Councillor Sobrinho-Wheeler, Councillor Simmons. AR 2026-16

items.title: Draft ordinance restricting use of City property/personnel for federal civil immigration enforcement
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the Law Department to draft ordinance language restricting the use of City-owned or City-controlled property, facilities, resources, and personnel for federal civil immigration enforcement, and to evaluate whether these protections should be added to the Welcoming Community Ordinance.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to direct the Law Department to draft ordinance language restricting the use of City-owned or City-controlled property, facilities, resources, and personnel for the purposes of federal civil immigration enforcement, and that the Law Department evaluate whether these protections should be adopted as an amendment to the Welcoming Community Ordinance. items.title: Update digital equity strategy and report by June 2026 on internet access programs
items.summary: Awaiting Report asking the City Manager to direct departments to review the city's digital equity work to date, update the evaluation of existing internet access programs, propose how to better meet residents' digital needs, and report back by June 2026.
items.body: The City Manager is requested to direct the appropriate departments to review the city's digital equity work to date, including the study's recommendations and all steps taken since the study conclusion in order to update the evaluation of existing internet access programs, assessing whether residents' digital needs are or could be better provided for and to propose how we can better meet their needs and to report back to the Council by June 2026 on internet access programs. Councillor Zusy, Councillor Nolan, Councillor Sobrinho-Wheeler, Vice Mayor Azeem. AR 2026-18
